feed.title: 4chan
feed.subtitle: Newest threads from selected worksafe 4chan boards (news, g, o, tv, v, mu, vip) via the read-only JSON API, plus the official 4chan blog. Board posts are user-generated and unmoderated; each title is prefixed with its board.

entry.title: /g/ Technology: One day AI is gonna scrape every ad profile, every tracker, every account you've…
entry.content: One day AI is gonna scrape every ad profile, every tracker, every account you've ever touched across the internet and stitch it into one neat dossier. Every embarrassing post, every search, every click. Good luck getting hired when HR can pull your entire digital life in five seconds. Public humiliation as a service. Enjoy the future.

entry.title: /g/ Technology: Dumpster Audio Help
entry.content: Found a pair of Mirage OM-5s in the dumpster with the boards also removed but nearby. Basically, everything is there with the exception of one of the subwoofer mesh covers. Initial testing: >I hooked both of them up, sent some sound through them via a good AVR. They sounded great... until the light on the front of each went red after about 30 minutes. Once this happens, perhaps it's in standby mode, but I cannot get the subwoofers to work again like this. >I do a total power off on the tower, wa

entry.title: /o/ Auto: How can you tell how close you are to a wall when you're back parking? I know al…
entry.content: How can you tell how close you are to a wall when you're back parking? I know almost all new cars have back and even front cameras but mine doesn't and it's longer than the average car so how do I park it without looking like an idiot and getting out multiple times from the car? I definitely don't want to make contact with the wall. I haven't done this in a while since when I drive to the shop there's always plenty of space and it's in open air and in my front of my house there's plenty of space

I only want to host a small blog to write some reviews and journal stuff im learning or doing at the moment, so the cheapest sub 10 euros vps will be enough for me >inb4 self host i tried but my IPS won't let me port forward t.eurocel if that even matters

entry.title: /s4s/ Sh*t 4chan Says: I don't often eat ice cream As nice as it is, let's face it, it's an overpriced …
entry.content: I don't often eat ice cream As nice as it is, let's face it, it's an overpriced luxury. And luxuries are getting harder and harder to afford. So when I do have ice cream, I just pick one I know I'll like, like chocolate or caramel or cookie dough or whatever. I don't really take risks or diversify. But recently, there was some discounted ice cream at Lidl. I decided to get two tubs. The first was caramel, but the second, as I had a back up option, I decided to try rum and raisin. I have to say I

entry.title: /tv/ TV & Film: i watch this every year on July 1st on the anniversary of the day i brought my d…

entry.title: /int/ International: Why would you want to experience every corner of the world?
entry.content: It's a child-like ambition, really. Not asinine, but very much naïve. I had dreams of visiting Poland, Japan, China, Australia... likely several more, yet I am okay with simply living in a local bubble. My ideal lifestyle involves living in a mobile home by myself, maybe in rural Iowa, with enough cash to never work again (at a modest lifestyle cost). Because even the "grounded" modern world is nested in layers of pointless abstractions, sometimes contradicting each other.

entry.title: /g/ Technology: Is it just me or has there been a major uptick in people getting hacked lately? …
entry.content: Is it just me or has there been a major uptick in people getting hacked lately? I've been noticing since like May with everyone losing their Gmail, Discord, Twitter, YouTube, Steam, etc. All this shit is apparently stemming from third/fourth worlders on Telegram who are selling off other people's accounts they've stolen just so they can recycle the same handful of scams onto other people. >MrBeast's brand new crypto casino >Hey I accidentally reported your Steam account >Watch anime with me on t

entry.title: /bant/ International/Random: When I was sixteen, I won a great victory. I felt in that moment I would live to…
entry.content: When I was sixteen, I won a great victory. I felt in that moment I would live to be a hundred. Now I know I shall not see thirty. None of us know our end, really, or what hand will guide us there. A king may move a man, a father may claim a son, but that man can also move himself, and only then does that man truly begin his own game. Remember that howsoever you are played or by whom, your soul is in your keeping alone, even though those who presume to play you be kings or men of power. When you

entry.title: /g/ Technology: Freenet
entry.content: >The new Freenet (distinct from the Java-based Hyphanet fork of original Freenet) is a Rust-implemented peer-to-peer global key-value store and decentralized computing platform. >Keys are WebAssembly contracts that define (1) validity predicates for values (e.g. signature checks), (2) update authorization rules, and (3) synchronization logic expressed as commutative monoid operations on state so that concurrent updates converge to the same result regardless of order. Each contract is determinist

entry.title: /g/ Technology: I want to share a simple mod I made for my 8BitDo Arcade Stick. I added a dedica…
entry.content: I want to share a simple mod I made for my 8BitDo Arcade Stick. I added a dedicated Up button with a switch that lets me choose between using the joystick or the button instead of having both active at the same time. The reason I did this is because I had trouble hitting certain combos consistently. Using the joystick for Up wasn't always accurate enough for me, especially during fast inputs. I saw that many people either convert their stick into a Hitbox or simply wire an Up button in parallel

entry.title: /g/ Technology: Retro tech will become popular again
entry.content: I honestly see in 5-10 years a revival of 1950-1990 analogue tech. Digital will devolve into something so bad people will return to dumb phones or printed books or physical cartridges of roms. School tests will be done on manual calculators and typewtiters.
